clause.text: If a credit card is not activated within 30 days of issuance, the issuer must seek OTP-based consent; absent consent, the account must be closed without cost within 7 working days of seeking confirmation.

check.description: Days-since-issuance comparison gating an OTP-consent workflow; days-since-confirmation comparison gating forced closure.

candidate_locus.note: card-activation batch program in GnuCOBOL-native base; CardDemo has no activation workflow

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD

clause.text: For an unsolicited card issued/activated and billed, the issuer must reverse the charges and pay the recipient a penalty of twice the value of the charges reversed.

current_value.note: Fixed statutory multiplier (2x charges reversed), not a directional threshold: a stale value is simply WRONG, not laxer/stricter on a grid. mo1_mode=exact_wrong with declared wrong-set [1], not [1,3]: the drift-direction principle extends to constants on two grounds - (a) D1 is temporal staleness and penalty provisions ratchet upward (every primary-verified change in this corpus moved toward strictness), so the plausible FORMER value sits below; (b) survival bias - drift that overpays (3x) is noticed and corrected internally, drift that underpays (1x) generates no internal signal and persists. The mechanism is indifferent to threshold-vs-constant.

check.description: Penalty computation: penalty = 2 * reversed_charges.

candidate_locus.note: COMPUTE statement in a penalty/adjustment batch program; GnuCOBOL-native base

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD

clause.text: The sanctioned credit limit shall not be breached at any point in time without explicit consent from the cardholder.

check.description: Over-limit gate on transaction posting: projected balance vs ACCT-CREDIT-LIMIT, with consent flag required to permit breach.

candidate_locus.note: shipped overlimit check; consent-flag dimension absent in CardDemo (itself a candidate D2)

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD

clause.text: Closure requests must be honoured within 7 working days (subject to dues); failure incurs a penalty of Rs 500 per calendar day of delay until closure, provided no outstanding.

penalty_per_day.note: Floor: minimum penalty the bank owes per calendar day. Stale side is DOWN (1-2-5 ladder: 500 -> 200) - an un-updated older rule pays the customer less. This leaf is why BL-15 exists; comparator now declared, never inferred.

check.description: Working-day window computation for closure SLA; per-calendar-day penalty accrual on breach.
check.t6_note: PAIR CONFIRMED primary-both-sides: 'per day' (2022) vs 'per calendar day' (2025 para 19). Differential: working-day accrual code = defensible under 2022 wording, drift under 2025. Annotation guidelines must carry an 'ambiguous-old-side' stance.

candidate_locus.note: closure batch program computing penalty accrual; GnuCOBOL-native base; existing pilot flag is a natural MO-6 host

prior_2022.note: calendar-day basis introduced by the 2024-03-07 amendment to the 2022 MD
prior_2022.text: Failure on the part of the card-issuers to complete the process of closure within seven working days shall result in a penalty of ₹500 per day of delay payable to the customer, till the closure of the account provided there is no outstanding in the account.

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD

clause.text: If a card is unused for more than one year, initiate closure after intimating the cardholder; if no reply within 30 days, close the account (subject to dues) and update the CIC within 30 days.

inactivity_threshold.note: Trigger: closure workflow fires when unused for MORE THAN one year. Stale side is UP (later closure of dormant accounts = laxer).

check.description: Inactivity-duration comparison driving a closure workflow; account-status transition and CIC notification deadlines. NB: 2025 para 20 is unused-card closure (the 2022 MD para 20 was co-branding outsourcing) -- the renumbering that seeded the earlier clause-20/29 confusion.

candidate_locus.note: the naturally-missing ACCT-ACTIVE-STATUS check (T0.2 D2 locus) is the code-side face of closed/blocked-account enforcement

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); value primary vs repealed 2022 MD

check.description: Interest-base construction: the balance on which interest is computed must exclude unpaid charges/levies/taxes; MAD formula must cover at least accrued interest+charges.

check.description: Strictly-greater-than-3-days comparison gating penal action (canonical > vs >= host); late-charge base variable selection (outstanding vs total-amount-due).
check.t6_note: P2 RETIRED (Phase 2, primary evidence): as-issued 2022 already contains 'more than three days' AND 'levied only on the outstanding amount after the due date and not on the total amount'. clause.text: The instructions/Directions on KYC/AML/CFT issued by RBI from time to time shall be strictly adhered to in respect of all cards issued, including co-branded cards.

check.description: Incorporation-by-reference bridge: makes RBI KYC Directions binding on card issuers. 2025 para 90 (was clause 29 in the repealed 2022 MD; NOT clause 20, which was co-branding outsourcing).

candidate_locus.note: para-90 is an incorporation-by-reference bridge, not a checkable-value clause; no longer an MO-4/D4 host. D4 (stale reference data) is now anchored to KYC-ovd-list (5(xiv)) and KYC-unsc-screening (56), whose current_value carries a genuine enum_set reference set (T2.4b, BL-6).

check.verification: 2025 paragraph mapping primary-confirmed against cc-dc-directions-2025.pdf (T2.5 Phase 2, zero corrections); bridge text primary vs repealed 2022 MD

clause.text: Periodic updation of KYC shall be carried out at least once every two years for high-risk, once every eight years for medium-risk, and once every ten years for low-risk customers, from the date of account opening or last KYC updation.

high_risk.note: Inverted CC-10a trap: the English says 'at least once every N years' (floor on FREQUENCY) but this leaf stores the INTERVAL, so the encoded quantity is a ceiling (at_most) and stale snaps UP (refreshing less often = laxer). Discriminator is the encoded quantity, NOT the clause's phrasing - 'at least' produced a floor on CC-10a and a ceiling here; pattern-matching on the words gets exactly one of them right.

low_risk.note: 10y is the loosest interval the lineage ever ran: no laxer value exists, so MO-1 rejects rather than emit the strict side.

check.description: Years-since-last-KYC comparison branched on risk category. Values stable across the 2016->2025 lineage: good D1 host, NOT a T6 pair.

candidate_locus.note: KYC-refresh scheduling batch; GnuCOBOL-native base (per T0.2 fit decision)

check.verification: primary (kyc-directions-2025.pdf, sha256-pinned; values re-confirmed in 2025 text)

prior_versions.text: Where the customer is a partnership firm, the beneficial owner is the natural person(s), who, whether acting alone or together, or through one or more juridical person, has/have ownership of/entitlement to more than 15 per cent of capital or profits of the partnership.
evidence: kyc-md-2016-final-consol.pdf footnote 8 on this sub-clause: 'Amended vide circular DOR.AML.REC.44/14.01.001/2023-24 dated October 17, 2023' - the SOLE footnote marker on sub-clause (b), so it was amended exactly once in the 2016-2025 lineage; 15% therefore held from issue to 2023-10-16

Bonus delta for annotation: the 2023 amendment ALSO added the 'or who exercises control through other means' limb - old-side code implementing pure percentage logic is conformant-2022, doubly drifted now (value + missing control limb).

prior_versions.verification: primary (insertion footnote proves absence of predecessor)

clause.text: 'Officially Valid Document (OVD)' means the passport, the driving licence, proof of possession of Aadhaar number, the Voter's Identity Card issued by the Election Commission of India, the job card issued by NREGA duly signed by an officer of the State Government, and the letter issued by the National Population Register containing details of name and address.

check.description: Presented customer identity document code screened against the enumerated OVD reference set at onboarding; an accepted-document table that drifts from para 5(xiv) admits an invalid document or rejects a valid OVD.

candidate_locus.note: authentic 88-level accepted-document table (ACCEPTED-OVD) consumed in the 2000-SCREEN-OVD onboarding gate; MO-4 corrupts one enumerated code -> stale reference data scorable against 5(xiv)

check.verification: clause_id 5(xiv) + the six-member OVD enumeration primary-confirmed against kyc-directions-2025.pdf (Track B, T2.4b D4 re-anchor per BL-6)
check.drift_story: MO-4 drops an OVD code that para 5(xiv) enumerates, so the accepted-document table no longer admits a valid Officially Valid Document; scored directly against the in-clause enumeration.

clause.text: it does not have any account in the name of individuals / entities appearing in the lists of individuals and entities, suspected of having terrorist links, which are approved by and periodically circulated by the United Nations Security Council (UNSC).

current_value.note: clause-side set = the two mandated UNSC list identities named in para 56; member entries are UNSC-maintained externally

check.description: Applicant list-source screened against the mandated UNSC sanctions-list registry; a screening registry that drops a mandated list (1267 or 1988) is stale reference data under para 56 (screening duty at para 17).

candidate_locus.note: authentic 88-level mandated-list registry (MANDATED-LIST) consumed in the 2000-SCREEN-SANCTIONS gate; MO-4 corrupts the Taliban(1988) source -> a mandated list unscreened

check.verification: para 56 UAPA/UNSC screening obligation primary-confirmed against kyc-directions-2025.pdf; clause_id 56(prevention) is the RAG chunk carrying the substantive 'no account for listed individuals/entities' obligation that introduces the two mandated lists (56(i) ISIL/Al-Qaida 1267/1989/2253, 56(ii) Taliban 1988). Track B, T2.4b.
check.drift_story: MO-4 drops the registry entry standing for the Taliban Sanctions List (para 56(ii)), so one of the two mandated UNSC lists is no longer screened -- a missing mandated list. The code-side identifier is a synthetic registry code, so the comparison is against registry completeness (both mandated sources present) rather than list membership.
